text: I feel hidden, as though I am peering into a world only partly revealed to me. My perspective is cramped, mostly blocked by cardboard and plastic, granting only a tantalizing glimpse of what lies beyond: tangled cables, a structure of dark interlocking pieces, the faint glow of monitors. The partial view invites curiosity and a sense of intrigue, but also a spark of frustration at being unable to see the whole picture. This limited vantage point makes me aware of my boundaries, both literal and metaphorical.
